tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Pickleball Player Sprints Into Burning House, Rescues Three Strangers
stories.summary: David Hester dropped his paddle mid-rally at Hillhurst Park and sprinted into flames to carry out a sleeping 13- or 14-year-old, then rushed back twice more for others—construction worker, no rescue training, pure instinct.

stories.headline: Young Pitcher Wins MLB Debut on Lou Gehrig Day, Honoring Father Lost to ALS
stories.summary: Chad Dallas made his MLB debut on Lou Gehrig Day, the exact anniversary of the tribute to the man who died of ALS—the same disease that took his father Tony one year ago.

stories.headline: Man Walks Across Texas, Delivers Nearly 100 Pairs of Shoes to Little League Kids
stories.summary: Andrew Zamarippas pushed a wagon hundreds of miles despite dehydration to gift free shoes to almost 100 young baseball players—determined generosity in motion.

stories.headline: Crested Ibis Population Soars From 7 Birds to 12,000 in 45-Year Triumph
stories.summary: From seven wild crested ibises discovered in Shaanxi Province in 1981, the species has rebounded to over 12,000 birds across 15 regions in 45 years of devoted protection.

stories.headline: All Four Curlew Chicks Hatch Safely After Firefighters Rescue Eggs From Wildfire
stories.summary: Northern Irish firefighters held back a wildfire just a metre from a curlew nest, and all four eggs they rescued have now hatched safely—a small miracle amid the flames.

stories.headline: South Florida Community Rallies to Restore Elderly Couple's Deteriorating Home
stories.summary: Volunteers from across the region came together to rebuild Edward and Clara Knox's home, celebrated with a ribbon-cutting that felt like a genuine homecoming.

stories.headline: First AI-Designed Vaccine Passes Human Trials, Protects Against Multiple Coronaviruses
stories.summary: British researchers successfully tested an entirely AI-created vaccine in humans, triggering safe immune responses against multiple viruses—a landmark breakthrough.

stories.headline: University Student Directs Traffic to Clear Path for Emergency Ambulance
stories.summary: Tuan Nurhan Syafiy stopped his motorcycle to guide an ambulance through gridlocked traffic in Malaysia, ensuring a patient reached hospital in time.

stories.headline: Indigenous Fire Management Brings Koalas Back Across Australian National Park
stories.summary: The Southern-Yuin Firesticks team's Indigenous fire management has brought koalas back to multiple locations in Wadbilliga National Park, led by Cultural Fire Practitioners Koorin Campbell and Durramah Parsons Campbell.

stories.headline: Stranger With Pressure Washer Tank Heroically Stops Car Fire on Interstate
stories.summary: Grant Belk pulled over with his pressure washing tank and discovered it still held water—exactly what he needed to help a panicking driver extinguish an SUV fire on the interstate.

stories.headline: Mangrove Forests Worldwide Shift From Decline to Recovery After 40 Years
stories.summary: After losing 2,900 square kilometers over 30 years, the world's mangrove forests shifted to net gain around 2010, with closed-canopy mangroves growing nearly 20% healthier between the 1980s and 2023.

stories.headline: Cubs Outfielder Redeems Costly Error With Walk-Off Single, Ends Home Losing Streak
stories.summary: Pete Crow-Armstrong, a 22-year-old Cubs rookie, turned a brutal inside-the-park home run error into walk-off redemption, delivering his first career walk-off single to snap Chicago's eight-game home losing streak.
